meaning: adj.可减少的,可约的,可还原的,可简化的
examples: The problem is not reducible to one of money.
The structure of the universe may not be reducible to a problem in physics.
Clearly, these two perspectives are not easily reconcilable, but the historical reality is not reducible to one or the other.
The networked marketplace is not reducible to the companies and governments that provide its wired and wireless infrastructure.
By now it is so established that it is reducible to jokes that depend entirely on the conventionality of conventional wisdom.
